mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and discount future cash flows, directly reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment stays below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in the residential real estate sector, which amplifies consumer confidence and drives positive equity market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation where CPIAUCSL crosses above 200, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, causing higher interest rate expectations that compress equity multiples and produce negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Market returns turn positive in >50% of high-inflation observations, or the predictive relationship reverses when CPIAUCSL falls below 195.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or positive production growth coincides with negative equity returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls cross above 200,000 additions in a single month, it confirms strong labor demand that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive equity market performance over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months where payroll growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real existing home sales signals broad-based economic demand that boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth slows below 1.0 percent, or the combined signal predicts negative returns in >50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where unemployment crosses below 4.5 percent, rising wage pressures constrain industrial production growth and trigger profit margin compression, resulting in negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >55% of observations during tight labor regimes, or the negative relationship breaks down when industrial production growth exceeds 2.0 percent.
